name: Civil Damages for Unauthorized Enticement of Information

eligibility.description: Applies when a US officer/employee entices an attorney, CPA, or enrolled agent to disclose privileged taxpayer information in exchange for compromising the professional's own tax issues.

actionability.retroactive_note: Action required during tax year — verify if still applicable

description: Taxpayers can recover up to $500,000 in damages if a federal employee intentionally compromises a tax professional to obtain the taxpayer's confidential information.
